findings.description: clamp_to_range does not verify all three datetimes share tz-awareness, so a mixed naive/aware input raises an unhandled TypeError from the comparison rather than the documented ValueError.
business_days_between materializes the entire day_range list just to count weekdays, allocating O(n) memory for what could be an O(1)-space counting loop.
day_range eagerly builds a full list, so very large spans (e.g. multi-millennium ranges) allocate millions of date objects with no generator/streaming alternative.
parse_iso_date docstring claims strict parsing but strptime accepts non-zero-padded components (e.g. '2024-1-5'), so the strictness contract is overstated.
humanize_delta has no upper cap beyond weeks, so a moment years in the past returns an unbounded value like '520 w ago' despite being a 'coarse human description'.
